text: Does the rough->fine granularity shift move the MEAN of held-out pass@k or its VARIANCE/reliability — is fine's benefit (if any) a uniform lift in per-problem solve probability, or a reduction in run-to-run variance (fewer wasted samples at fixed mean) — and does that mean-vs-variance split itself depend on model size? Buildable as a per-sample-outcome reanalysis of the planned q-0007 eval logs, no extra generations.
